hero.title: Enterprise-Grade Security for Democratic Elections
hero.subtitle: Five independent security layers protect every vote from click to count
hero.promise: Your vote is anonymous. Your election is secure. Your results are verifiable.
hero.ceo_tagline: Trusted by associations, non-profits, and enterprises worldwide
hero.ceo_description: Run secure online elections that your members trust. Zero compromises on anonymity, security, or verifiability.

architecture.title: Security Architecture
architecture.subtitle: Five independent layers protect every vote
architecture.image_alt: Five-layer security architecture diagram showing request validation, temporal validation, tenant isolation, business logic, and data persistence layers

layer1.title: Request Validation
layer1.description: Verifies voting link exists, belongs to user, and is active
layer1.explanation: We check that your voting link is real, belongs to you, and hasn't been stolen or deactivated.

layer2.title: Temporal Validation
layer2.description: Checks session expiration and election timing
layer2.explanation: Your voting session automatically expires after 24 hours, and votes are only accepted when the election is officially open.

layer3.title: Golden Rule Enforcement
layer3.description: Ensures voter belongs to correct organization
layer3.explanation: You can only vote in elections from your own organization. Other organizations' elections are completely invisible to you.

layer4.title: Business Logic
layer4.description: Validates voting rules (one person, one vote)
layer4.explanation: The system ensures you can only vote once, your code is valid, and you're selecting real candidates.

layer5.title: Data Persistence
layer5.description: Stores votes anonymously with cryptographic proof
layer5.explanation: We literally cannot see how you voted. Your vote is stored with no connection to your identity, but you can still verify it was counted.

summary: Five layers of security mean your vote is protected at every step. Even if one layer were compromised, the other four still protect you.

anonymity.title: Complete Anonymity
anonymity.description: No voter IDs stored with votes. Votes are completely anonymous while still being verifiable.
anonymity.tagline: Even we don't know how you voted

verification.title: Cryptographic Verification
verification.description: SHA256 hashes prove votes were counted without revealing voter identity.
verification.tagline: You can verify without revealing your vote

isolation.title: Multi-Tenant Isolation
isolation.description: Each organization's data is completely separated and independent.
isolation.tagline: Your data stays with your organization

journey_subtitle: Complete protection through five secure steps
journey_image_alt: Voting journey flowchart showing five security checkpoints

badges.anonymity_desc: No voter ID stored with votes

question1.question: How do you keep my vote anonymous?
question1.answer: We don't store any voter identification with your vote. Our votes table has no user_id column. Votes contain only candidate selections and are identified only by cryptographic hashes, making voter-vote linkage mathematically impossible.

question2.question: What prevents someone from voting twice?
question2.answer: Our system enforces "one person, one vote" through multiple layers: voting codes are one-time use only, voting sessions are tied to specific users, and voting history is tracked. No code can be used twice.

question3.question: Can organizations see each other's data?
question3.answer: No. Each organization's data is completely isolated at the database level. Organizations cannot cross-query or access other organizations' elections, voters, or results under any circumstances.

question4.question: How can I verify my vote was counted?
question4.answer: You receive a unique cryptographic hash of your vote immediately after voting. You can verify this hash is included in the final results without revealing your vote contents to anyone else.
